paper_title: Pollen Analysis and Differentiation in Manuka and Kanuka

methods.description: Process for collecting pollen from manuka and kanuka plants and preparing it for imaging and analysis. This method ensures that pollen samples are prepared consistently to produce optimal images for analysis.

steps.description: Pollen was collected from mature anthers of two individuals of L. scoparium and K. ericoides, and from one sample of monofloral manuka honey.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from plants and honey

steps.description: The pollen was acetolyzed, suspended in silicone oil, and mounted on glass slides.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples using acetolysis and mount on slides

steps.description: The slides were placed on a Classifynder, which automatically locates and images all pollen grains present on the slide.
steps.searchable_summary: use Classifynder to image pollen grains

steps.description: Images of pollen grains oriented in polar and equatorial views were manually extracted from the image set produced by the Classifynder.
steps.searchable_summary: extract polar and equatorial view images of pollen grains

methods.description: Method for measuring the size and shape of pollen grains using image analysis techniques. This method provides quantitative data on pollen morphology, which is crucial for differentiating between manuka and kanuka pollen.

steps.description: Pollen size is captured through two different measurements: the area of the pollen grain, and maximum Feret diameter (MFD).
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen grain area and maximum Feret diameter

steps.description: Measurements of shape used include elongation factor, compactness factor, convex hull, Heywood circularity factor, and hydraulic radius.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate shape measurements for pollen grains
